flex justify-between items-center min-h-[72px] py-rem-8"><p class="typography-module_body-m-compact-bold__Y0Jyw">When was the Suzuka Circuit built?</p><div class="f1rd-open hidden"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1em" height="1em" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" role="img"><title>Chevron Up</title><path fill="currentColor" d="m18 14-6-6-6 6 1.4 1.4 4.6-4.6 4.6 md:gap-rem-16 lg:gap-rem-24"><p class="typography-module_body-s-regular__ul7F5 typography-module_md_body-m-regular__d9VRg typography-module_lg_body-l-regular__dPa5z">In 1962. Soichiro Honda, whose car company you may have heard of, was a man with big ambitions. Seeking to turn Honda into an automotive powerhouse, he decided his company should have its own test track. Dutchman John Hugenholtz got the nod, and drew up the now-iconic ‘crossover’ Suzuka track – although original drawings saw the track pass over and under itself a full three times, which would have been sweet!</p></div></div></div></details><details name=":R2qn7rrrrncv8kq:" class="[&[open]_.f1rd-open]:block [&[open]_.f1rd-closed]:hidden border-t-thin [&:first-child]:border-t-0 border-surface-neutral-4" data-f1rd-a7s-click="qanda_interaction" data-f1rd-a7s-context="eyJhY3Rpb25UeXBlIjoib3BlbiIsImNsaWNrVGV4dCI6IldoZW4gd2FzIGl0cyBmaXJzdCBHcmFuZCBQcml4PyJ9"><summary class="cursor-pointer list-none [&::-webkit-details-marker]:hidden flex justify-between items-center min-h-[72px] py-rem-8"><p class="typography-module_body-m-compact-bold__Y0Jyw">When was its first Grand Prix?</p><div class="f1rd-open hidden"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1em" height="1em" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" role="img"><title>Chevron Up</title><path fill="currentColor" d="m18 14-6-6-6 6 1.4 1.4 4.6-4.6 4.6 4.6z"></path></svg></div><div class="f1rd-closed"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1em" height="1em" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" role="img"><title>Chevron Dropdown</title><path fill="currentColor" d="m18 9.4-6 6-6-6L7.4 8l4.6 4.6L16.6 8z"></path></svg></div></summary><div class="mb-rem-16 pb-rem-8"><div class="px-px-24 max-w-para-lg"><div class="flex flex-col gap-rem-12 md:gap-rem-16 lg:gap-rem-24"><p class="typography-module_body-s-regular__ul7F5 typography-module_md_body-m-regular__d9VRg typography-module_lg_body-l-regular__dPa5z">Despite most people agreeing that Suzuka is a worthy successor to sliced bread in the ‘Best Thing’ stakes, the Japanese track was a relative latecomer to the F1 calendar, making its first appearance in 1987. Nigel Mansell would remember that weekend well, after he suffered a crash in qualifying that ruled him out of the race, gifting that year’s championship to his bitter rival Nelson Piquet.</p></div></div></div></details><details name=":R2qn7rrrrncv8kq:" class="[&[open]_.f1rd-open]:block [&[open]_.f1rd-closed]:hidden border-t-thin [&:first-child]:border-t-0 border-surface-neutral-4" data-f1rd-a7s-click="qanda_interaction" data-f1rd-a7s-context="eyJhY3Rpb25UeXBlIjoib3BlbiIsImNsaWNrVGV4dCI6IldoYXTigJlzIHRoZSBjaXJjdWl0IGxpa2U/In0="><summary class="cursor-pointer list-none [&::-webkit-details-marker]:hidden flex justify-between items-center min-h-[72px] py-rem-8"><p class="typography-module_body-m-compact-bold__Y0Jyw">What’s the circuit like?</p><div class="f1rd-open hidden"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1em" height="1em" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" role="img"><title>Chevron Up</title><path fill="currentColor" d="m18 14-6-6-6 6 1.4 1.4 4.6-4.6 4.6 4.6z"></path></svg></div><div class="f1rd-closed"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1em" height="1em" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" role="img"><title>Chevron Dropdown</title><path fill="currentColor" d="m18 9.4-6 6-6-6L7.4 8l4.6 4.6L16.6 8z"></path></svg></div></summary><div class="mb-rem-16 pb-rem-8"><div class="px-px-24 max-w-para-lg"><div class="flex flex-col gap-rem-12 md:gap-rem-16 lg:gap-rem-24"><p class="typography-module_body-s-regular__ul7F5 typography-module_md_body-m-regular__d9VRg typography-module_lg_body-l-regular__dPa5z">Show us a racing a driver who doesn’t love Suzuka, and we’ll show you a liar. The high-speed track remains one of the ultimate driving challenges, with the snaking ‘S’ Curves, the two commitment-rewarding Degners and the white-knuckle ride of 130R all highlights in a series of highlights at what is one of F1’s seminal tracks. And hey, it’s got a crossover, which is always cool, right?</p></div></div></div></details><details name=":R2qn7rrrrncv8kq:" class="[&[open]_.f1rd-open]:block [&[open]_.f1rd-closed]:hidden border-t-thin [&:first-child]:border-t-0 border-surface-neutral-4" data-f1rd-a7s-click="qanda_interaction" data-f1rd-a7s-context="eyJhY3Rpb25UeXBlIjoib3BlbiIsImNsaWNrVGV4dCI6IldoeSBnbz8ifQ=="><summary class="cursor-pointer list-none [&::-webkit-details-marker]:hidden flex justify-between items-center min-h-[72px] py-rem-8"><p class="typography-module_body-m-compact-bold__Y0Jyw">Why go?</p><div class="f1rd-open hidden"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1em" height="1em" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" role="img"><title>Chevron Up</title><path fill="currentColor" d="m18 14-6-6-6 6 1.4 1.4 4.6-4.6 4.6 4.6z"></path></svg></div><div class="f1rd-closed"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1em" height="1em" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" role="img"><title>Chevron Dropdown</title><path fill="currentColor" d="m18 9.4-6 6-6-6L7.4 8l4.6 4.6L16.6 8z"></path></svg></div></summary><div class="mb-rem-16 pb-rem-8"><div class="px-px-24 max-w-para-lg"><div class="flex flex-col gap-rem-12 md:gap-rem-16 lg:gap-rem-24"><p class="typography-module_body-s-regular__ul7F5 typography-module_md_body-m-regular__d9VRg typography-module_lg_body-l-regular__dPa5z">A track that has witnessed an unfair share of legendary F1 moments (not least between Messrs Prost and Senna), a unique set of fans with an unabashed commitment to playing Formula 1 dress-up, a Ferris wheel, and great food. Okay, for most of us it’s a long slog to get there, but surely a better question is: ‘Why not go?’</p></div></div></div></details><details name=":R2qn7rrrrncv8kq:" class="[&[open]_.f1rd-open]:block [&[open]_.f1rd-closed]:hidden border-t-thin [&:first-child]:border-t-0 border-surface-neutral-4" data-f1rd-a7s-click="qanda_interaction" data-f1rd-a7s-context="eyJhY3Rpb25UeXBlIjoib3BlbiIsImNsaWNrVGV4dCI6IldoZXJlIGlzIHRoZSBiZXN0IHBsYWNlIHRvIHdhdGNoPyJ9"><summary class="cursor-pointer list-none [&::-webkit-details-marker]:hidden flex justify-between items-center min-h-[72px] py-rem-8"><p class="typography-module_body-m-compact-bold__Y0Jyw">Where is the best place to watch?</p><div class="f1rd-open hidden"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1em" height="1em" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" role="img"><title>Chevron Up</title><path fill="currentColor" d="m18 14-6-6-6 6 1.4 1.4 4.6-4.6 4.6 4.6z"></path></svg></div><div class="f1rd-closed"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1em" height="1em" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" role="img"><title>Chevron Dropdown</title><path fill="currentColor" d="m18 9.4-6 6-6-6L7.4 8l4.6 4.6L16.6 8z"></path></svg></div></summary><div class="mb-rem-16 pb-rem-8"><div class="px-px-24 max-w-para-lg"><div class="flex flex-col gap-rem-12 md:gap-rem-16 lg:gap-rem-24"><p class="typography-module_body-s-regular__ul7F5 typography-module_md_body-m-regular__d9VRg typography-module_lg_body-l-regular__dPa5z">Take your pick. Grandstands D and E overlook the ‘S’ Curves, giving you an amazing chance to watch downforce and commitment working in perfect harmony as the drivers negotiate the sinuous turns. Elsewhere, the left flank of Grandstand G places you at the inside of 130R, which should be a bit of a treat, while if it’s overtaking you want, Grandstand I will give you a view of the incident-attracting Hairpin.</p></div></div></div></details></div></div></div></div></div></div><div class="Container-module_container__0e4ac colors-module_bg_colour-surface-neutral-surface-neutral-3__u3lwa"><div class="Container-module_inner__UkLYJ"><div class="flex flex-col py-px-48 lg:py-px-64 gap-px-48 lg:gap-px-64"><h2 class="typography-module_display-2-xl-black__WHPIk Text-module_upper__pWFEw">JAPAN</h2><ul class="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-px-8 md:gap-px-16 lg:gap-px-24 items-stretch" data-f1rd-a7s-context="eyJsb2NhdGlvbkluUGFnZSI6IlJhY2UgUHJvbW90aW9uYWwgQXJ0aWNsZXMifQ=="><li data-f1rd-a7s-click="promotional_content_click" data-f1rd-a7s-context="" class="DynamicArticleCard-module_card__GNumG DynamicArticleCard-module_overlay__CqnD6 DynamicArticleCard-module_constrained__m-x-r DynamicArticleCard-module_surface-neutral-1__Yj6F6 md:col-span-2" aria-describedby="title:S3:"><span class="DynamicArticleCard-module_image__N42Rw"><img src="https://media.formula1.com/image/upload/t_16by9North/c_fit,w_1584/q_auto/v1740000001/fom-website/2026/Japan/GettyImages-2268873747.webp" alt="SUZUKA, JAPAN - MARCH 29: Pierre Gasly of France driving the (10) Alpine F1 A526 Mercedes leads Max Verstappen of the Netherlands driving the (3) Oracle Red Bull Racing RB22 Red Bull Ford on track during the F1 Grand Prix of Japan at Suzuka Circuit on March 29, 2026 in Suzuka, Japan. Soichiro Honda, whose car company you may have heard of, was a man with big ambitions. Seeking to turn Honda into an automotive powerhouse, he decided his company should have its own test track. Dutchman John Hugenholtz got the nod, and drew up the now-iconic ‘crossover’ Suzuka track – although original drawings saw the track pass over and under itself a full three times, which would have been sweet!\n\n### When was its first Grand Prix?\n\nDespite most people agreeing that Suzuka is a worthy successor to sliced bread in the ‘Best Thing’ stakes, the Japanese track was a relative latecomer to the F1 calendar, making its first appearance in 1987. Nigel Mansell would remember that weekend well, after he suffered a crash in qualifying that ruled him out of the race, gifting that year’s championship to his bitter rival Nelson Piquet.\n\n### What’s the circuit like?\n\nShow us a racing a driver who doesn’t love Suzuka, and we’ll show you a liar. The high-speed track remains one of the ultimate driving challenges, with the snaking ‘S’ Curves, the two commitment-rewarding Degners and the white-knuckle ride of 130R all highlights in a series of highlights at what is one of F1’s seminal tracks. And hey, it’s got a crossover, which is always cool, right?\n\n### Why go?\n\nA track that has witnessed an unfair share of legendary F1 moments (not least between Messrs Prost and Senna), a unique set of fans with an unabashed commitment to playing Formula 1 dress-up, a Ferris wheel, and great food. Okay, for most of us it’s a long slog to get there, but surely a better question is: ‘Why not go?’\n\n### Where is the best place to watch?\n\nTake your pick. Grandstands D and E overlook the ‘S’ Curves, giving you an amazing chance to watch downforce and commitment working in perfect harmony as the drivers negotiate the sinuous turns. Elsewhere, the left flank of Grandstand G places you at the inside of 130R, which should be a bit of a treat, while if it’s overtaking you want, Grandstand I will give you a view of the incident-attracting Hairpin."])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"36:{\"text\":\"Practice 1\",\"value\":\"practice?meeting=1281\u0026session=1\",\"isSessionResult\":true,\"isAvailable\":true,\"editorialSessionType\":\"Practice1\"}\n37:{\"text\":\"Practice 2\",\"value\":\"practice?meeting=1281\u0026session=2\",\"isSessionResult\":true,\"isAvailable\":true,\"editorialSessionType\":\"Practice2\"}\n38:{\"text\":\"Practice 3\",\"value\":\"practice?meeting=1281\u0026session=3\",\"isSessionResult\":true,\"isAvailable\":true,\"editorialSessionType\":\"Practice3\"}\n39:{\"text\":\"Qualifying\",\"value\":\"qualifying?meeting=1281\",\"isSessionResult\":true,\"isAvailable\":true,\"editorialSessionType\":\"Qualifying\"}\n3f:{\"public_id\":\"common/f1/2026/mercedes/andant01/2026mercedesandant01right\",\"default_image\":\"common/f1/2026/fallback/driver/2026fallbackdriverright\"}\n40:{\"public_id\":\"common/f1/2026/mercedes/2026mercedeslogowhite\"}\n3e:{\"completionStatusCode\":\"OK\",\"racePoints\":25,\"version\":\"Final\",\"gapToLeader\":\"0\",\"gapToPrevious\":null,\"lapsBehindLeader\":\"0\",\"positionValue\":\"1\",\"positionNumber\":\"1\",\"lapsCompleted\":\"53\",\"raceTime\":\"1:28:03.403\",\"racingNumber\":\"12\",\"constructorSeasonName\":\"Mercedes\",\"driverId\":1161,\"driverFirstName\":\"Kimi\",\"driverLastName\":\"Antonelli\",\"driverShortName\":\"K. was the Suzuka Circuit built?\",\"content\":\"In 1962. Soichiro Honda, whose car company you may have heard of, was a man with big ambitions. Seeking to turn Honda into an automotive powerhouse, he decided his company should have its own test track. Dutchman John Hugenholtz got the nod, and drew up the now-iconic ‘crossover’ Suzuka track – although original drawings saw the track pass over and under itself a full three times, which would have been sweet!\"},{\"heading\":\"When was its first Grand Prix?\",\"content\":\"Despite most people agreeing that Suzuka is a worthy successor to sliced bread in the ‘Best Thing’ stakes, the Japanese track was a relative latecomer to the F1 calendar, making its first appearance in 1987. Nigel Mansell would remember that weekend well, after he suffered a crash in qualifying that ruled him out of the race, gifting that year’s championship to his bitter rival Nelson Piquet.\"},{\"heading\":\"What’s the circuit like?\",\"content\":\"Show us a racing a driver who doesn’t love Suzuka, and we’ll show you a liar. The high-speed track remains one of the ultimate driving challenges, with the snaking ‘S’ Curves, the two commitment-rewarding Degners and the white-knuckle ride of 130R all highlights in a series of highlights at what is one of F1’s seminal tracks. And hey, it’s got a crossover, which is always cool, right?\"},{\"heading\":\"Why go?\",\"content\":\"A track that has witnessed an unfair share of legendary F1 moments (not least between Messrs Prost and Senna), a unique set of fans with an unabashed commitment to playing Formula 1 dress-up, a Ferris wheel, and great food. Okay, for most of us it’s a long slog to get there, but surely a better question is: ‘Why not go?’\"},{\"heading\":\"Where is the best place to watch?\",\"content\":\"Take your pick. Grandstands D and E overlook the ‘S’ Curves, giving you an amazing chance to watch downforce and commitment working in perfect harmony as the drivers negotiate the sinuous turns. Elsewhere, the left flank of Grandstand G places you at the inside of 130R, which should be a bit of a treat, while if it’s overtaking you want, Grandstand I will give you a view of the incident-attracting Hairpin.\"}]},\"raceReview\":{\"raceResults\":[{\"driverTLA\":\"ANT\",\"driverFirstName\":\"Kimi\",\"driverLastName\":\"Antonelli\",\"teamName\":\"Mercedes\",\"positionNumber\":\"1\",\"raceTime\":\"1:28:03.403\",\"teamColourCode\":\"27f4d2\",\"gapToLeader\":\"0\",\"driverNameFormat\":\"FirstNameIsPrimary\"},{\"driverTLA\":\"PIA\",\"driverFirstName\":\"Oscar\",\"driverLastName\":\"Piastri\",\"teamName\":\"McLaren\",\"positionNumber\":\"2\",\"raceTime\":\"1:28:17.125\",\"teamColourCode\":\"ff8000\",\"gapToLeader\":\"13.722\",\"driverNameFormat\":\"FirstNameIsPrimary\"},{\"driverTLA\":\"LEC\",\"driverFirstName\":\"Charles\",\"driverLastName\":\"Leclerc\",\"teamName\":\"Ferrari\",\"positionNumber\":\"3\",\"raceTime\":\"1:28:18.673\",\"teamColourCode\":\"e8002d\",\"gapToLeader\":\"15.27\",\"driverNameFormat\":\"FirstNameIsPrimary\"}]},\"assemblyVideoListByTag\":{\"tags\":[{\"id\":\"F1:Race Text-module_upper__pWFEw\",\"children\":\"About\"}],[\"$\",\"$L85\",null,{\"items\":[{\"id\":\"0\",\"title\":\"When was the Suzuka Circuit built?\",\"content\":[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"px-px-24 max-w-para-lg\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L86\",null,{\"text\":\"In 1962. Soichiro Honda, whose car company you may have heard of, was a man with big ambitions. Seeking to turn Honda into an automotive powerhouse, he decided his company should have its own test track. Dutchman John Hugenholtz got the nod, and drew up the now-iconic ‘crossover’ Suzuka track – although original drawings saw the track pass over and under itself a full three times, which would have been sweet!\"}]}]},{\"id\":\"1\",\"title\":\"When was its first Grand Prix?\",\"content\":[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"px-px-24 max-w-para-lg\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L86\",null,{\"text\":\"Despite most people agreeing that Suzuka is a worthy successor to sliced bread in the ‘Best Thing’ stakes, the Japanese track was a relative latecomer to the F1 calendar, making its first appearance in 1987. Nigel Mansell would remember that weekend well, after he suffered a crash in qualifying that ruled him out of the race, gifting that year’s championship to his bitter rival Nelson Piquet.\"}]}]},{\"id\":\"2\",\"title\":\"What’s the circuit like?\",\"content\":[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"px-px-24 max-w-para-lg\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L86\",null,{\"text\":\"Show us a racing a driver who doesn’t love Suzuka, and we’ll show you a liar. The high-speed track remains one of the ultimate driving challenges, with the snaking ‘S’ Curves, the two commitment-rewarding Degners and the white-knuckle ride of 130R all highlights in a series of highlights at what is one of F1’s seminal tracks. And hey, it’s got a crossover, which is always cool, right?\"}]}]},{\"id\":\"3\",\"title\":\"Why go?\",\"content\":[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"px-px-24 max-w-para-lg\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L86\",null,{\"text\":\"A track that has witnessed an unfair share of legendary F1 moments (not least between Messrs Prost and Senna), a unique set of fans with an unabashed commitment to playing Formula 1 dress-up, a Ferris wheel, and great food. Okay, for most of us it’s a long slog to get there, but surely a better question is: ‘Why not go?’\"}]}]},{\"id\":\"4\",\"title\":\"Where is the best place to watch?\",\"content\":[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"px-px-24 max-w-para-lg\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L86\",null,{\"text\":\"Take your pick. Grandstands D and E overlook the ‘S’ Curves, giving you an amazing chance to watch downforce and commitment working in perfect harmony as the drivers negotiate the sinuous turns. Elsewhere, the left flank of Grandstand G places you at the inside of 130R, which should be a bit of a treat, while if it’s overtaking you want, Grandstand I will give you a view of the incident-attracting Hairpin.\"}]}]}]}]]}]]}]}]}]}],[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"Container-module_container__0e4ac colors-module_bg_colour-surface-neutral-surface-neutral-3__u3lwa\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"Container-module_inner__UkLYJ\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"flex flex-col py-px-48 lg:py-px-64 gap-px-48 lg:gap-px-64\",\"children\":[[[\"$\",\"h2\",null,{\"className\":\"typography-module_display-2-xl-black__WHPIk Text-module_upper__pWFEw\",\"children\":\"JAPAN\"}],[[\"$\",\"ul\",null,{\"className\":\"grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-px-8 md:gap-px-16 lg:gap-px-24 items-stretch\",\"data-f1rd-a7s-context\":\"eyJsb2NhdGlvbkluUGFnZSI6IlJhY2UgUHJvbW90aW9uYWwgQXJ0aWNsZXMifQ==\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"li\",null,{\"data-f1rd-a7s-click\":\"promotional_content_click\",\"data-f1rd-a7s-context\":\"\",\"className\":\"DynamicArticleCard-module_card__GNumG DynamicArticleCard-module_overlay__CqnD6 DynamicArticleCard-module_constrained__m-x-r DynamicArticleCard-module_surface-neutral-1__Yj6F6 md:col-span-2\",\"aria-describedby\":\"title:S3:\",\"children\":[[\"$\",\"span\",null,{\"className\":\"DynamicArticleCard-module_image__N42Rw\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"img\",null,{\"src\":\"https://media.formula1.com/image/upload/t_16by9North/c_fit,w_1584/q_auto/v1740000001/fom-website/2026/Japan/GettyImages-2268873747.webp\",\"alt\":\"SUZUKA, JAPAN - MARCH 29: Pierre Gasly of France driving the (10) Alpine F1 A526 Mercedes leads Max Verstappen of the Netherlands driving the (3) Oracle Red Bull Racing RB22 Red Bull Ford on track during the F1 Grand Prix of Japan at Suzuka Circuit on March 29, 2026 in Suzuka, Japan.
